This calculator determines your zinc dosage by age and sex. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed periodically to keep you informed.

Teaching and learning zinc nutrition concepts
explanation:
## Reference Values in Health

Normal reference values come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. These are guidelines only—your personal medical history always takes priority.

## How This Calculator Works

This calculator determines your zinc dosage based on age and gender using established RDA (Recommended Dietary Allowance) standards. It applies the formula to your input values and alerts you if results fall outside typical ranges.

## Normal Reference Values for Adults

| Parameter | Normal Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Body Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| SpO2 (Blood Oxygen) | 95-100% |
| Fasting Blood Sugar | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL Cholesterol | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL Cholesterol | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions—medical, financial, or legal—consult a qualified healthcare professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How much zinc do I need per day?
faq.a: The RDA is 11 mg for adult men, 8 mg for adult women, and 11-13 mg during pregnancy.
Zinc supports immunity, wound healing, testosterone production, and normal taste and smell function.

faq.q: Can zinc help prevent or shorten a cold?
faq.a: Taking 30-75 mg of zinc in the first few days of a cold may help reduce its duration.

faq.q: What form of zinc is best?
faq.a: Zinc picolinate, gluconate, and citrate are better absorbed than zinc oxide.

faq.q: Should I take zinc with food?
faq.a: Yes, zinc is better absorbed with food. Avoid taking it with coffee or foods high in tannins, which can reduce absorption.

faq.q: What's the maximum safe zinc intake?
faq.a: The upper limit is 40 mg per day for long-term use. Higher doses can interfere with copper absorption.

faq.q: What foods are good sources of zinc?
faq.a: Red meat, oysters, pumpkin seeds, and legumes are excellent sources of bioavailable zinc.

faq.q: When should I talk to my doctor about zinc?
faq.a: Consult a healthcare provider if your values are outside normal ranges, you experience symptoms, or you have a family history of cardiovascular or metabolic disease.
